title: Codonoboea hirsuta
taxonomy: Codonoboea hirsuta is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This plant is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Indonesia, Malaysia, and the Philippines. It is a woody climber with hairy stems and leaves, which is its common name's basis. The Gesneriaceae family is a large and diverse family of flowering plants, often known as the "African violet" family. Codonoboea hirsuta is classified within this family due to its shared characteristics with other Gesneriaceae species.
